ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operations" (
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operation */,
  "operation_type" TEXT /* Type of the operation (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operation */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the operation was created */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the operation */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the operation (e.g., pending, in progress, completed, failed) */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message associated with the operation */,
  "completed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the operation was completed */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the transaction associated with this operation */,
  "is_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the operation is sensitive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operations_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation this parameter is associated with */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "parameter_type" TEXT /* Type of the parameter (e.g., string, integer, struct) */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is required */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., int, float, string) */,
  "max_length" INTEGER /* Maximum length for string parameters */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value for numeric parameters */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value for numeric parameters */,
  "allowed_values" TEXT /* List of allowed values for the parameter */,
  "is_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is sensitive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation associated with this transaction */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the transaction (e.g., committed, rolled back) */,
  "commit_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the transaction was committed */,
  "rollback_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the transaction was rolled back */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the transaction */,
  "is_rollback_possible" INTEGER /* Indicates if the transaction can be rolled back */,
  "rollback_reason" TEXT /* Reason for rolling back the transaction */,
  "is_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the transaction is sensitive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, auditor) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed version of the user's password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation associated with this log */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message describing the log entry */,
  "log_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log entry was created */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the operation */,
  "operation_status" TEXT /* Status of the operation at the time of logging */,
  "is_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the log entry is sensitive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameter_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter setting */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parameter this setting is associated with */,
  "parameterize" INTEGER /* Indicates whether to use cfqueryparam for this parameter */,
  "reload" INTEGER /* Indicates whether to reload the data before operation */,
  "is_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter setting is sensitive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameter_settings_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id")
)
